intent: We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS AI coding agent with a two-process architecture (Swift shell + Python backend) that uses dual-model consensus AI (Claude + GPT-4o) to autonomously decompose build intents into ordered PRDs and PRs, generate implementation and test code, run self-correction/lint/fix loops, execute CI, and gate on operator approval. The system encompasses 16 TRD-specified subsystems: macOS application shell with Touch ID/Keychain/XPC (TRD-1), consensus engine with parallel generation and Claude arbitration (TRD-2), confidence-gated build pipeline with pr_type routing (TRD-3), multi-agent coordination via GitHub-stored ledger (TRD-4), GitHub integration with rate limiting and CI workflow management (TRD-5), holistic 5-lens code review (TRD-6), adaptive TRD development workflow with FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT modes (TRD-7), full SwiftUI design system with health dashboard and remediation stream (TRD-8), self-hosted Mac CI runner infrastructure (TRD-9), FAISS-backed document store with injection defense (TRD-10), comprehensive security threat model (TRD-11), backend startup/shutdown sequence with version handshake (TRD-12), three-layer recovery and state management with build memory and self-improving build rules (TRD-13), seven-fix CI false-positive pipeline (TRD-14), operational runbook (TRD-15), FM-taxonomy testing with 35 regression tests (TRD-16), and self-healing autonomous observability and remediation loop (TRD-17). Five additional subsystems identified as gaps (TRD-18 Crafted Connect SDK, Figma connector, deployment infrastructure, email notification, and webhook receiver) will be resolved during PRD decomposition using best practices as confirmed by operator.

Clarifications:
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices. This needs to be a configurable inout in the app
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - on by default, fall back if not discovered
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - TRD-18 was combined with TRD-17

Operator on 'Is this build intent for the full system (all 17 TRDs from s':
build intent for the full system

Operator on 'The repo already contains a v38.153.0 codebase with 30+ Pyth':
generate a fresh implementation

Operator on 'TRD-17 (Self-Healing) and TRD-8 v3.0 (Health Dashboard) both':
TRD-18 was combined with TRD-17

Operator on 'The Figma design pipeline (TRD-8 §14, §24) references Figma ':
yes. Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.

prd_plan.title: Foundation, Monorepo Bootstrap, Versioning, Configuration, and Shared Contracts
macOS Native Shell, App Lifecycle, Touch ID/Keychain Auth, Settings, and Backend Process Host
Authenticated XPC/Unix Socket Channel, Wire Protocol, Handshake, and Command Routing
Backend Runtime Startup, Ready Handshake, Degraded States, and Graceful Shutdown
Document Store, FAISS Retrieval, Embeddings, Injection Defense, and Product Knowledge Loading
Consensus Engine, Dual-Model Provider Adapters, Prompt Systems, Arbitration, and Native Output Enforcement
GitHub Integration, Repository Bootstrap, PR Lifecycle, Rate Limiting, and CI Workflow Management
Webhook Receiver, Event Ingestion, and Polling Fallback
Confidence-Gated Build Pipeline, PRD/PR Planning, pr_type Routing, and Execution Orchestration
Repo Context Fetch, Self-Correction Loop, Lint Gate, Failure Classification, and Local Fix Loop
Multi-Agent Coordination, Build Ledger, Journals, Conflict Detection, and Live Sync
Recovery, Thread State, Build Memory, Build Rules, Context Management, and Resume Semantics
Holistic Five-Lens Code Review Workflow and Fix PR Generation
Adaptive TRD Development Workflow with FOUNDER, ENGINEER, and CONSULTANT Modes
SwiftUI Design System, Build Stream, Health Dashboard, Remediation UX, and Figma Pipeline
Security Threat Model Enforcement, Path Safety, Audit Logging, and Operational Runbook
Mac CI Runner Infrastructure, Swift Validation Workflow, Signing, Notarization, and Release Artifacts
Testing Framework, FM-Taxonomy Regression Suite, and End-to-End Validation
Observability, Self-Healing Loop, Autonomous Remediation, Crafted Connect SDK, and Embedded Issue Reporting

1.title: Scaffold repository directory layout and root files
1.summary: Creates the canonical monorepo directory layout with all top-level directories and root files. Includes VERSION (0.1.0), .gitignore, .env.example, AGENTS.md, LICENSE, Makefile with stub targets, and placeholder READMEs to preserve empty dirs in Git for forge-docs/, forge-standards/, scripts/, .github/workflows/.

2.title: Add architecture standards and conventions documents
2.summary: Creates the four canonical standards documents in forge-standards/: ARCHITECTURE.md (two-process architecture, module boundaries), INTERFACES.md (XPC contract shapes, API surface), CONVENTIONS.md (naming, file layout, import ordering, docstring format), DECISIONS.md (ADR log template with first entry for monorepo choice), and build_rules.md template with seed rules for version sync, lint-before-commit, test-before-merge.

3.title: Add Python project metadata, dependency manifests, and toolchain config
3.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/__init__.py, tests/__init__.py, and .github/workflows/README.md to establish the Python package and test directory tree. Ensures Git tracks all required paths for the Python backend.

4.title: Add Swift package manifest and Xcode project skeleton
4.summary: Introduces the Crafted/ and CraftedTests/ directory structure with minimal Swift entry files (CraftedApp.swift, AppRootView.swift), Info.plist, Version.xcconfig, and Xcode project metadata placeholder. Establishes the Swift shell side of the two-process architecture.

5.title: Add Python package skeleton, version module, and shared domain enums
5.summary: Creates pyproject.toml with version dynamically reading from VERSION file, requirements.txt with pinned runtime deps (pyyaml, pydantic), requirements-dev.txt (pytest, ruff, mypy, pre-commit), and root conftest.py that inserts src/ into sys.path for pytest import resolution. Configures pytest settings, ruff rules, and mypy strict mode in pyproject.toml.

6.title: Define shared error taxonomy and exception hierarchy
6.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/version.py that reads VERSION at import time and exposes __version__ string. Creates scripts/sync_version.py that reads VERSION and patches pyproject.toml version field and Crafted/Support/Info.plist CFBundleShortVersionString. Creates Crafted/Support/GeneratedVersion.swift with version constant. Adds Makefile target 'version-sync'. Script is idempotent and fails with non-zero exit if target files are missing.

7.title: Define shared data schemas — PRD, PR, BuildIntent, CIResult, HealthStatus
7.summary: Implements src/forge_agent/errors.py with hierarchical ForgeError base exception, subsystem-specific error classes (ConfigError, ConsensusError, PipelineError, GitHubError, XPCError, CIError, DocumentStoreError, SecurityError, RecoveryError), and severity enum. Implements src/forge_agent/enums.py with shared enumerations: BuildPhase, PRType, ConfidenceBand, AgentMode (FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT), HealthStatus, CIStatus, ConsensusStrategy, and ReviewLens.

8.title: Define XPC message protocol types and operator I/O settings (Python side)
8.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/contracts/ package with xpc_schemas.py defining typed Pydantic message schemas for Swift–Python XPC communication: HandshakeRequest, HandshakeResponse (with version field), CommandEnvelope, ResponseEnvelope, HealthPing, HealthPong, ErrorPayload. Creates primitives.py for shared atomic types (ConfidenceScore, Timestamp). All schemas support JSON round-trip serialization.

9.title: Tests for shared contracts — serialization round-trips and stability
9.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/contracts/build_schemas.py with typed Pydantic models for build pipeline contracts: PRDSpec, PRSpec (with pr_type routing field), BuildIntent, BuildResult, ConfidenceScore, ConsensusVote, ReviewResult, CIResult, and OperatorApproval. These are the data contracts that flow through the confidence-gated pipeline. All models use enums from PR-7.

10.title: Implement typed configuration models with validation and secret exclusion
10.summary: Introduces Codable Swift contract models and error definitions that mirror the Python shared contracts for shell–backend interoperability: AgentEnums.swift, AgentErrors.swift, AgentMessage.swift (CommandEnvelope, ResponseEnvelope, Handshake types), AgentPrimitives.swift. Field names and enum cases match Python definitions exactly.

11.title: Implement configuration loader with layered resolution and environment override
11.summary: Adds golden tests that verify shared contract identifiers, serialized field names, and enum coverage remain aligned between Python and Swift definitions. Python side generates a manifest JSON of all contract names/fields; Swift side validates against the same manifest. Catches drift between the two language representations.

12.title: Add source-controlled configuration templates and environment examples
12.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/config/ package with models.py containing Pydantic BaseModel classes: RuntimeConfig, BuildConfig, AIProviderConfig (endpoints, model names, temperature, max_tokens — no secrets), GitHubConfig (owner, repo, rate limit params), CIConfig (runner labels, timeout, retry), ObservabilityConfig (log level, metrics port, trace sampling), SelfHealingConfig (enabled toggle, max retries, cooldown), and FeatureToggles (consensus_enabled, touch_id_required, ci_gating). All fields typed with defaults and validators. No secret values in defaults.

13.title: Tests for configuration precedence, secrets policy, and I/O behavior
13.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/config/loader.py that loads configuration from layered sources: defaults → config file (YAML) → environment variables. Reads FORGE_CONFIG_PATH for YAML file location, applies env var overrides with FORGE_ prefix. Creates src/forge_agent/config/validation.py for cross-field validation and secret rejection (rejects keys matching *_SECRET, *_TOKEN, *_API_KEY in YAML). Creates src/forge_agent/config/redaction.py for safe config serialization that masks sensitive fields. Returns frozen ForgeConfig root model. Includes default config.yaml resource.

14.title: Define Swift shared domain enums and error taxonomy mirroring Python
14.summary: Creates src/forge_agent/config/settings_surface.py defining the SettingsSurface model — all operator-configurable inputs (AI provider selection, consensus toggle, CI gating, Touch ID requirement, approval mode, notification preferences) with descriptions, types, defaults, and validation. Creates src/forge_agent/config/operator_io.py for operator-resolved ambiguity inputs. This is the canonical registry of tunable knobs.

15.title: Define Swift XPC contract models and I/O settings mirroring Python
15.summary: Focused regression tests for: non-secret defaults validation, environment override precedence over YAML over defaults, invalid value rejection, secret redaction in emitted config views, cross-field validation rules, and frozen config immutability.

16.title: Add Swift configuration models and decoding support
16.summary: Adds .pre-commit-config.yaml with hooks for ruff format, ruff check --fix, mypy, trailing whitespace, end-of-file fixer, and VERSION sync check. Adds .ruff.toml if additional config beyond pyproject.toml is needed. Ensures all tooling config is consistent with pyproject.toml settings from PR-5.

17.title: Add cross-language contract fixture tests for Swift-Python parity
17.summary: Creates scripts/bootstrap.sh: checks Python ≥3.11, creates venv, installs requirements, runs version sync, installs pre-commit hooks, verifies tooling availability, prints success banner. Creates scripts/check_env.py that validates required env vars from .env.example (warns on missing, errors on security-critical). Creates scripts/bootstrap_swift.sh for macOS Swift prerequisites. Updates Makefile 'bootstrap' target. All scripts are idempotent.

18.title: Implement version synchronization tooling for Python and Swift metadata
18.summary: Creates .github/workflows/crafted-ci.yml: triggers on push/PR to main, runs ruff check, ruff format --check, mypy, pytest with coverage, and version sync verification. Creates .github/workflows/crafted-ci-macos.yml: triggers on push/PR, runs Swift build and CraftedTests on macos-latest runner. Both use concurrency groups to cancel stale runs.

19.title: Add developer bootstrap script and local validation commands
19.summary: Validates workflow file presence and required structure, bootstrap script behavior contracts (idempotency, error on missing Python), and baseline repository assumptions for contributors and CI.

20.title: Add Makefile with standard dev commands and pre-commit hooks
20.summary: Updates ARCHITECTURE.md with finalized module boundaries reflecting actual code structure. Updates INTERFACES.md with concrete XPC schema references. Updates DECISIONS.md with ADR entries for config layering, contract parity approach, and error taxonomy. Updates CONVENTIONS.md with import ordering and docstring examples from actual codebase. Updates build_rules.md with CI-enforced rules.

21.title: Add CI workflow skeleton for lint, type-check, test, and Swift build
21.summary: Integration tests validating the complete foundation layer: VERSION file, pyproject.toml version, and version.py __version__ all agree; ForgeConfig constructs from defaults, overrides by env vars, serializes to/from JSON; all contract schemas round-trip through JSON; error hierarchy is correct (all errors inherit ForgeError); settings surface entries have valid defaults that pass config validation; repository structure matches required layout.

22.title: Add developer setup guide, configuration policy, and operator config reference
22.summary: Create README.md with project overview and quickstart. Create docs/DEVELOPER_SETUP.md with step-by-step local setup instructions referencing bootstrap.sh and Makefile targets. Create docs/CONFIGURATION_POLICY.md documenting the layered config strategy and secret handling rules. Create forge-standards/OPERATOR_CONFIG_GUIDE.md documenting every configurable input/output, its default, override mechanism, and which subsystem it affects. Create docs/VERSIONING.md explaining the single-source VERSION strategy.
